segments.text: We are going to derive this function using two different paths, the first will be
considering it as it is, that is, applying the rule of the chain for powers and the other
alternative will be transforming first all this expression to then proceed to
derive it. Then we start with the first option, we have here an expression
raised to a numerical exponent where we are going to use the rule of the chain for powers,
we are going to remember the model, if we have apple to the n, the apple is all this expression,
n is number 2, then the derivative of that expression will be n by
apple to the n minus 1 and all this
multiplied by the derivative of the apple, this is what is called the rule of the chain for powers
and this is the internal derivative, the derivative of what we have in the base.
Then, following this instruction, the derivative of that function, which is denoted as g prime of x,
will be equal to the following,
lower the exponent n, that is, the 2, this multiplied by the apple without deriving,
